title: paphia woodsii
intro: Paphia woodsii, also known as Woods' Paphia, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Paphia, which is a type of terrestrial orchid. It is native to New Zealand and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and fight off infections. The leaves and roots of the plant can be made into herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions, respiratory problems, and digestive issues.
